youtube_metadata.description: Obscure computing history, retro console hacks, and tech relics from the 80s, 90s, and 2000s. #shorts #retrotech

fallback_facts.yt_search_query: floppy disk drive 3.5 disk reader old computer
fallback_facts.text_block: The standard save icon in modern software is a three-and-a-half-inch <yellow>floppy disk</yellow> which held only one point four four megabytes. A single average photo today would require <red>three floppies</red> to store.
fallback_facts.title: The Save Icon

fallback_facts.yt_search_query: nintendo game boy screen pixel gameplay pocket
fallback_facts.text_block: The original Game Boy possessed only <yellow>eight kilobytes</yellow> of RAM. Despite this, programmers created legendary, complex, and full-length titles that fit into <red>tiny game cartridges.</red>
fallback_facts.title: Game Boy RAM Limits
